quizzes.title: Using Air Scrubbers and AFDs to Improve Job Site Air Quality — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: According to the course, HEPA filters are capable of capturing what percentage of airborne particles that are 0.3 microns or larger?
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Components and Mechanics of AFDs

questions.questionText: Which formula does the course give for calculating Air Changes per Hour (ACH)?
questions.sourceRef: M6 — Calculating Air Changes per Hour (ACH)

questions.questionText: An air scrubber delivers 500 CFM in a room with a volume of 10,000 cubic feet. Using the course formula, what is the ACH?
questions.sourceRef: M6 — Calculating Air Changes per Hour (ACH)

questions.questionText: Which mode of operation is used when the goal is containment of contaminants?
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Modes of Operation: Recirculation vs Negative Pressure

questions.questionText: What is a key limitation of recirculation mode described in the course?
options: It does not control the direction of airflow and should not be relied on for containment
It cannot capture fine dust particles
It requires ducting to vent air outside the space
It only functions when an activated carbon filter is fitted
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Modes of Operation: Recirculation vs Negative Pressure

questions.questionText: What is the recommended ACH range for mould remediation?
questions.sourceRef: M6 — Calculating Air Changes per Hour (ACH)

questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of the pre-filter in an air scrubber?
options: To absorb VOCs and neutralise odours
To deactivate microbes using UV light
To capture larger particles and protect the HEPA filter downstream
To create a pressure differential for containment
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Types of Filters and Their Functions

questions.questionText: Which instrument is used to verify that negative pressure has been achieved in a containment setup?
